title: creating temp table kills performance of the original query
poc: create table t1 as (select * from test1()) with data;
create table t2 as (select distinct v1 from t1) with data;
select count(t1.v1) from t1, t2 where t1.v1=t2.v1;
key_bug_features: creating temp table affects original query performance
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test1 (v1 INT, v2 VARCHAR(50), v3 DATE);
INSERT INTO test1 (v1, v2, v3) VALUES (1, 'test', '2023-01-01'), (2, 'example', NULL), (3, 'sample', '2023-03-01');
CREATE TABLE t1 AS (SELECT * FROM test1) WITH DATA;
CREATE TABLE t2 AS (SELECT DISTINCT v1 FROM t1 WHERE v2 IS NOT NULL) WITH DATA;
SELECT COUNT(t1.v1) FROM t1 JOIN t2 ON t1.v1 = t2.v1 WHERE t1.v3 > '2023-01-01';
